w:r.w:t: Complete endpoint documentation with request/response examples
All protected endpoints require a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
You get the access_token from any auth endpoint (Google, Apple, or Phone OTP). Pass it in every request that says "Auth Required: Yes".
No auth required for any of these endpoints (except signout).
Sign in using a Google ID token obtained from Google Sign-In on the mobile app.
The ID token returned by Google Sign-In SDK on the mobile device
Sign in using an Apple ID token obtained from Sign in with Apple on the mobile app.
The identity token returned by Apple Sign-In SDK
Same structure as Google sign-in response (access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, user).
Sends a one-time password (OTP) SMS to the given phone number. Phone must be in E.164 format.
Verifies the OTP received via SMS. Returns session tokens on success.
Same phone number used in send-otp
Same structure as Google sign-in response.
Use the refresh_token from a previous login to get a new access_token when it expires (every 1 hour).
The refresh token from a previous login or refresh response
Same structure as Google sign-in response.
Invalidates the current session. The access token is no longer valid after this.
Returns the full profile of the currently authenticated user.
No request body. No query params.
Returns the profile of any user by their UUID. Used to view other users' profiles.
UUID of the user to fetch
Same structure as Get Own Profile response.
Called once after a new user's first sign-in to complete their individual profile setup. Sets onboarding_completed to true.
Allowed: everyone, men, women, women_only (default: everyone)
Called once for personal trainers, coaches, and fitness professionals to complete their profile.
"bio": "Certified personal trainer with 5 years of experience helping clients achieve their goals.",
Update one or more profile fields. Only send the fields you want to change.
Uploads a profile photo to Supabase Storage. Replaces any existing photo.
Image file (JPG, PNG). Max size: 10 MB.
Note: Do NOT send JSON for this endpoint. Use multipart/form-data with the file attached as the "photo" field.
Registers the device's push notification token (FCM for Android, APNs via FCM for iOS). Call this after login and whenever the token refreshes.
The FCM registration token from the device

Records today's fitness activity with a single tap. Idempotent — calling it multiple times on the same day returns the same result without creating duplicates. Automatically awards streak badges at 7, 30, and 90 days.
Note: new_badge will be null if no badge was earned today. If already checked in today, response will include "already_checked_in": true.
Returns the user's current streak, longest streak, and total check-ins.
No request body. No query params.
Returns all streak badges earned by the user. Badge types: streak_7 (7-day streak), streak_30 (30-day streak), streak_90 (90-day streak).
No request body. No query params.
Returns the user's check-in dates for the last 30 days. Use this to render a calendar/heatmap view.
No request body. No query params.
Returns a list of users sorted by compatibility score (0-100). Excludes users you have already liked or matched with. Applies distance filtering using Haversine formula.
Filter by a specific goal tag e.g. weight_loss
Filter by gender: everyone, men, women
Express interest in another user. If the other user has already liked you, a mutual match is created automatically and a push notification is sent to both users.
UUID of the user you want to like
Success Response (200) — Like sent, no match yet
UUID of the user to unlike
Returns a paginated list of all mutual matches for the current user.
Returns the list of all users you have liked (regardless of whether they liked you back).
Permanently removes a mutual match. The other user will no longer appear in your matches list.
UUID of the match to delete
Browse user cards on the Explore screen People tab. Same scoring and filtering as discovery but includes all users (not filtered by likes/matches).
Returns upcoming community fitness events shown in the Explore screen Events tab. Only returns events that have not ended yet.
"description": "Join us for a community 5K run in Lodhi Garden.",
Search for users by name or fitness goal tag. Minimum 2 characters required.
Search query — matches user names or fitness goal tags (min 2 chars)
All endpoints require authentication. Only matched users can message each other.
Returns all active conversations sorted by most recent message. Used to render the chat list screen.
No query params. No request body.
Returns paginated message history for a specific match conversation. Messages are returned oldest-first within each page.
Sends a text message to the matched user. The recipient receives a push notification. Supabase Realtime delivers the message instantly to connected clients.
Marks all unread messages in a conversation as read. Call this when the user opens a chat screen.
Returns in-app notification history for the current user (matches and new messages).
Marks all unread notifications as read in one call.
Marks a specific notification as read.
Auth Required: Yes + Admin role (is_admin = true in profile)
All admin endpoints return 403 Forbidden if the user is not an admin.
Returns a paginated list of all users with optional filters. Max 100 per page.
Full user detail including badges, last 30 check-ins, total match count, and moderation status.
Permanently bans a user. Also disables their account in Supabase Auth. All their sessions are invalidated.
Reason for the ban (stored in admin logs)
Removes a ban and re-enables the user's account in Supabase Auth.
Temporarily suspends a user until a specified date.
Marks a professional profile as verified (is_verified = true). A verification badge is shown on their profile.
Permanently deletes a user and all their data (cascades via foreign keys). This cannot be undone.
Returns the moderation queue — reports filed by users against other users or messages.
Full details of a single report including context of the reported user or message.
URL Parameter: reportId — UUID of the report.
Success Response (200): Returns full report object with reporter, reported_user, and reported_message fields.
Marks a report as resolved and records the action taken.
URL Parameter: reportId — UUID of the report.
Action taken: warn, ban, delete_content, none
Dismisses a report as invalid or not actionable.
Shortcut to view only pending message reports (type=message, status=pending).
Key platform metrics snapshot — total users, DAU, MAU, matches, messages, and pending reports.
Returns the count of unique active users (users who checked in) per day for the last N days. Use for chart rendering.
Number of past days to return
Returns unique active users per month for the last N months.
Number of past months to return
Returns D1, D7, and D30 retention rates. For each cohort: how many users who signed up N days ago checked in yesterday.
Returns new user signups per day for the last N days.
Number of past days to return
Returns number of new matches created per day for the last N days.
Number of past days to return
Sends a push notification to all users or a filtered segment. Handles FCM (Android) and APNs via FCM (iOS). Stale/invalid tokens are auto-cleaned. Results are logged to broadcast_logs.
Optional segment filters — omit to send to all users
Target users with this goal tag
Optional key-value data payload (all values must be strings)
Request Body Example — Send to all users
"body": "Check out the new Events tab in Explore."
Request Body Example — Send to a segment
"body": "Join our 7-day beginner streak challenge starting tomorrow.",
Returns a log of all past broadcast notifications with delivery stats.
Returns all events (upcoming and past) for admin management.
Returns full details of a single event.
URL Parameter: eventId — UUID of the event.
Success Response (200): Returns full event object.
Creates a new community fitness event that appears in the Explore > Events tab.
URL to the event banner image
"description": "Community 5K run open to all fitness levels. Refreshments provided.",
Updates one or more fields of an existing event. Only send fields you want to change.
URL Parameter: eventId — UUID of the event.
Permanently deletes an event. It will no longer appear in the Explore screen.
URL Parameter: eventId — UUID of the event.
All endpoints return errors in the same format:
Missing required fields or invalid values in request body/query
Missing or invalid Authorization header / expired token
Valid token but insufficient permissions (e.g. non-admin accessing admin routes)
Resource (user, match, event, etc.) does not exist
Rate limit exceeded (200 req/min global, 20 req/15min for auth)
